description:
## What to build

Set up TauRPC as Beadsmith's typed Tauri RPC boundary for the first issue-list command. Wrap the pure Rust `bw` adapter from `bsm-8ul` behind a typed RPC function that returns the frontend-facing issue-list contract.

`IssueSummary` belongs to the RPC/frontend contract. Raw Beadwork JSON and any adapter-only parsing shape should stay hidden behind the adapter.

## Contract

The success payload should include:

- `workspacePath`: the absolute or process-resolved path used as the command working directory.
- `issues`: an array of `IssueSummary`.

`IssueSummary` should include these fields, preserving absent Beadwork values as empty strings, empty arrays, or omitted optional fields consistently:

- `id`
- `title`
- `status`
- `priority`
- `type`
- `assignee`
- `labels`
- `parent`
- `blockedBy`
- `blocks`
- `created`
- `updatedAt`
- `closedAt`
- `deferUntil`
- `due`

The error payload should distinguish at least command execution failure and JSON parsing failure. It should carry a concise message safe to show in the UI.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] TauRPC is installed and configured for the Tauri/React app.
- [ ] A typed RPC function exposes all issue summaries from the current workspace.
- [ ] The RPC function delegates to the Rust adapter rather than invoking `bw` directly.
- [ ] The TypeScript-facing success and error shapes are generated, documented, or otherwise discoverable without guessing.
- [ ] Missing `bw`, non-Beadwork directories, subprocess failures, and JSON parse failures remain distinguishable from a true empty issue list.
- [ ] Raw Tauri `invoke` is not introduced as the app integration pattern for this functionality.
- [ ] Relevant Rust/TypeScript generation checks pass.

## Triage

ready-for-agent
title: Expose issue summaries through TauRPC
